rag_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure that all visible identifiers are properly declared and accessible in the current scope.
If a validator reports a type error or unresolved identifier, trace the issue to the first invalid reference and fix or remove the offending clause.
generic_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the nature of the contract.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex when defining AGREE contracts.
Ensure that range bounds in guarantees are concrete values, named constants, or visible relations from the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must reference identifiers that are either AADL features or previously declared helpers in the same annex.
Guarantee expressions must have a valid type; avoid using undefined or untyped identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid referencing identifiers that are not visible in the current scope, such as non-existent packages or property sets.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
